title: Atlas — one biological-domain workspace inside atlas-core
description: Per ATLAS_INTEGRATION_PROMPT.md. An atlas is the unit of *biological-domain ownership* — inversion_atlas, meiosis_atlas, evolution_atlas, etc. It bundles a set of products, questions, hooks, and pages under one biological theme. The atlas itself is a registered descriptor, not a folder: a product knows which atlas it belongs to via `atlas`, and `atlases.jsonl` carries the human-facing context (label, color, lead question, primary products).

color.description: Hex color used as the accent in the sidebar / atlas card.

icon.description: Single emoji shown next to the atlas name.

lead_question_id.description: FK into questions.jsonl: the canonical question the atlas exists to answer.

primary_products.description: FK(s) into products.jsonl: the products this atlas is *meant* to publish.

depends_on_atlases.description: Other atlas_ids this one's products typically depend on (informational).

repo.description: Optional URL or path to the atlas repo (when split out).
